title: dianthus furcatus subsp. requienii
intro: Dianthus furcatus subsp. requienii, also known as Pineapple Candy or Pink Foliage, is a subspecies of the Dianthus furcatus plant, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to soothe and calm digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote respiratory health. The health benefits of Dianthus furcatus subsp. requienii include its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help to protect against infections and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent, allowing for the extraction of its medicinal compounds.
